sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Find orange diamond position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Orange objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        cr = sum(x[0] for x in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(x[1] for x in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  O at ({cr:.0f},{cc:.0f}) pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']}")

print("\nAll objects with small pixel counts:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 20 and n['pixels'] > 3:
        cr = sum(x[0] for x in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(x[1] for x in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  {n['color']} at ({cr:.0f},{cc:.0f}) pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click the orange diamond center
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 51, 'col': 40}])
print(f"Click diamond: done={result.get('done')}, level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nOrange objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O' and n['pixels'] > 10:
        cr = sum(x[0] for x in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(x[1] for x in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  O at ({cr:.0f},{cc:.0f}) pixels={n['pixels']}")

print("\nWhite objects (small):")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and 5 < n['pixels'] < 20:
        cr = sum(x[0] for x in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(x[1] for x in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  W at ({cr:.0f},{cc:.0f}) pixels={n['pixels']}")

print("\nDark gray objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] > 5:
        cr = sum(x[0] for x in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(x[1] for x in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  G at ({cr:.0f},{cc:.0f}) pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
